title: Make G3 a private append-only evidence gate that never self-signs
context: The user authorized continuing the full Constitution implementation after the first staged/working advisory slice. Gate G3 requires a green/yellow scorecard with no privacy red line, measured proof-card comprehension and review burden, executable conformance for at least three human-selected clients, and preregistered retrieval/compiler experiments, while separate human policy activation and gate sign-off remain mandatory.
decision: Represent G3 plans, EXP-01/EXP-03 preregistrations, proof-card review measurements, and adapter-conformance receipts as private, content-addressed, append-only artifacts with tamper and branched-supersession protection. Bind a G3 plan to the exact eligible G2 readiness receipt, the complete G2 policy set, exact current experiment revisions, and at least three human-selected client identifiers. Certify only the executable CLI/MCP/CI profile through the real end-to-end adapter fixture; unsupported client profiles fail closed. Derive a deterministic scorecard and emit only eligible_for_human_g3_signoff with authority:none and g3_passed:false. Advisory policy activation and final G3 sign-off remain separate explicit human actions.
consequences: Corrections immediately retract eligibility by superseding the current plan, experiment, review, or conformance receipt without deleting history.
Real G3 remains not_ready until a human selects the exact client trio and full policy packet, activates those policies advisory, completes current-card measurements, and separately signs off.
CLI and client-neutral MCP expose the same read-only readiness receipt; only the CLI can append explicit evidence or execute conformance.
The release gate now includes G3 readiness and the staged/working adapter fixture in its hashed test manifest.
alternatives_rejected: Use a mutable YAML/checklist sign-off: it cannot prove exact policy/proof/client/experiment bindings and permits history rewriting.
Let successful proof or conformance automatically activate advisory policies or pass G3: evidence is not authority and would violate the separate human activation requirement.
Treat arbitrary named client sets as certified because they share core code: labels are not executable conformance evidence, so unsupported profiles must remain errors.
Allow a cherry-picked subset of the G2 policy packet into G3: it would make review-burden and proof-currency scorecards gameable.
